name: Craig Musser
occupation: Manager, Environment, Health & Safety at PureField Ingredients
education: B.S., Bucknell University; B.S., Shippensburg University of Pennsylvania; M.S. in Occupational Safety / Safety Leadership, Eastern Kentucky University; M.B.A., Frostburg State University
whoTheyAre:
Craig Musser is a safety and environmental health manager running for Congress in Kansas's 1st District under the United Kansas Party, a new third party that earned official recognition as the state's fifth political party in May 2024. He lives near Ellinwood in Barton County, placing him squarely in the rural heart of the Big First -- unlike the two Democratic challengers who both reside in Lawrence.

Musser works at PureField Ingredients, a wheat protein and biofuels manufacturer in Russell, Kansas. His career spans decades of industrial operations across pharmaceutical, chemical, glass, and agricultural manufacturing, including stints at Merck & Co., Ply Gem, and Jacam Catalyst. He filed his FEC paperwork on February 14, 2026, and is running directly in the general election rather than going through a primary.
theirRecord:
Musser has no prior record in elected office. His candidacy is itself the record: a late-filed, third-party challenge under a brand-new banner in one of the most Republican districts in the country. The United Kansas Party, founded in 2024 by Jack Curtis, collected over 35,000 petition signatures to earn ballot access but has never won a race.

Musser's professional record is in industrial safety and environmental compliance. He has managed maintenance, health, and safety operations at multiple manufacturing facilities across the eastern United States and Kansas. His campaign messaging emphasizes practical governance and service over ideology, but with minimal infrastructure -- no major social media presence, limited news coverage, and no visible fundraising -- it remains a long-shot effort.
whatYouShouldKnow: Musser lives in Ellinwood (population roughly 2,000) in Barton County, making him the only KS-01 challenger who actually resides in the rural core of the district.

He is running under the United Kansas Party, a cross-partisan organization recognized as Kansas's fifth political party in May 2024. The party advocates for fusion voting and has filed lawsuits challenging Kansas laws that forbid multi-party nominations.

His campaign has very limited public infrastructure. As of early 2026, there is a campaign website and an FEC filing but no visible social media campaign accounts and no substantive fundraising data on the public record.

Musser's platform includes a statement on women's rights and self-determination, noting that "every Kansan, particularly women whose rights are being eroded by the incumbent party, has the right to self-determination and freedom from government intrusion into their personal lives." This is notable in post-Dobbs Kansas, where voters rejected an anti-abortion constitutional amendment 59-41 in August 2022.

He was originally educated in Pennsylvania, Maryland, and Kentucky before building his career in Kansas industrial manufacturing. He is not a Kansas native but has deep roots in the state's working economy.
whereTheyWorship: No public church affiliation was verified in the available research. No confirmed congregation name or church website was found tied to Musser.

quotes.text: Kansans deserve a representative who serves them -- not donors, party bosses or Washington insiders.
Effective governance is not about ideology -- it's about listening and delivering outcomes.
The long odds are exactly why this fight is so important.

campaignFinance.narrative: Musser filed his FEC Statement of Candidacy on February 14, 2026, establishing the committee "Craig Musser for Congress." No substantive fundraising totals, donor ledgers, or cash-on-hand figures have surfaced in the accessible public record. The campaign appears to be a very small, early-stage operation with limited fundraising visibility.
campaignFinance.undisclosed: No donor data available in publicly accessible filings as of April 2026.
campaignFinance.reportingPeriod: Filed February 14, 2026; no financial reports yet available
